notes: CODE reads the first UTF-16 code unit.
CODE spills over an array-valued text input on the current baseline.
CONCATENATE textifies ordinary scalar args.
CHAR spills over an array-valued numeric input on the current baseline.
FIND baseline.
Empty needle with in-range start returns the start position.
FINDB delegates to FIND on the current Unicode baseline.
LEFT baseline.
LEFTB delegates to LEFT on the current Unicode baseline.
LEN counts UTF-16 code units.
LENB delegates to LEN on the current Unicode baseline.
LOWER textifies logical TRUE.
LOWER spills over an array-valued text input on the current baseline.
MID baseline.
MIDB delegates to MID on the current Unicode baseline.
PROPER honors apostrophe word-boundary behavior on the current baseline.
REPLACE baseline.
REPLACEB delegates to REPLACE on the current Unicode baseline.
REPT truncates count toward zero.
REPT spills over an array-valued count input on the current baseline.
RIGHT baseline.
RIGHTB delegates to RIGHT on the current Unicode baseline.
SEARCH is case-insensitive on the current ASCII-seeded baseline.
SEARCH wildcard star behavior baseline.
SEARCHB delegates to SEARCH on the current Unicode baseline.
SUBSTITUTE instance_num baseline.
TRIM collapses ASCII spaces.
TRIM preserves non-breaking spaces.
TRIM spills over an array-valued text input on the current baseline.
TEXTAFTER spills over an array-valued instance_num on the current baseline.
TEXTBEFORE spills over an array-valued instance_num on the current baseline.
UNICODE reads the first scalar from the surrogate pair.
UNICODE returns the first scalar for precomposed text.
UPPER textifies logical TRUE.
UPPER spills over an array-valued text input on the current baseline.
CODE rejects empty text.
FIND returns #VALUE! when the needle is absent.
LEFT rejects negative counts.
MID rejects start_num below 1.
REPLACE rejects start_num below 1.
SEARCH rejects empty needle when the start position is past the end.
SEARCH returns #VALUE! when the pattern is absent.
REPT enforces the Excel text-length ceiling.
